name: v1_probe_gqa_r0
cmd: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate 0.0 --selector true_cls --benchmark gqa --subset eval/subsets/gqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_probe_v1/gqa_r0_metrics.json --max-tokens 32 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --seed 0
note: v1 TRUE-CLS-attn selector CONTROL on GQA (r=0). At r=0 the selector is a no-op (control), so this is byte-identical to the proxy r=0 baseline -- we re-run it as the speedup denominator FOR THE v1 SERIES so speedup ratios are computed within one selector family (avoids conflating selector change with prune-rate change). Note: r0 metrics SHOULD match the proxy probe's gqa_r0 within noise (sanity check).

name: v1_probe_gqa_r25
cmd: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate 0.25 --selector true_cls --benchmark gqa --subset eval/subsets/gqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_probe_v1/gqa_r25_metrics.json --max-tokens 32 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --seed 0
note: v1 GQA @ 25% prune (keep 432/576), REAL [CLS]->patch attn selector (diversity OFF, lam=0 -- the FasterVLM/VTC-CLS setting). PRIMARY accuracy comparison point vs proxy probe r25 (acc 0.555, -3.0%): expect true CLS-attn to tighten the drop.

name: v1_probe_gqa_r50
cmd: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate 0.50 --selector true_cls --benchmark gqa --subset eval/subsets/gqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_probe_v1/gqa_r50_metrics.json --max-tokens 32 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --seed 0
note: v1 GQA @ 50% prune (keep 288/576), REAL CLS-attn. THE GATE POINT for the v1 selector: proxy r50 was 0.565 (-2.0%); v1 should improve accuracy at iso-throughput (the throughput numbers should match the proxy r50 within noise -- same hook, same KV-cache win -- confirming the serving win is selector-agnostic, the accuracy is not).

name: v1_probe_gqa_r75
cmd: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate 0.75 --selector true_cls --benchmark gqa --subset eval/subsets/gqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_probe_v1/gqa_r75_metrics.json --max-tokens 32 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --seed 0
note: v1 GQA @ 75% prune (keep 144/576), REAL CLS-attn. Stress point: proxy r75 was 0.470 (-11.5%, the steep-drop region). True CLS-attn should recover meaningful accuracy here -- if it brings r75 to within ~6% of r0, the v1 method's accuracy story is strong and we can argue the proxy UNDERSTATED the method.

note: v1 TRUE-CLS CONTROL on TextVQA (r=0). OCR-heavy denominator for the v1 TextVQA series.

name: v1_probe_textvqa_r50
cmd: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate 0.50 --selector true_cls --benchmark textvqa --subset eval/subsets/textvqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_probe_v1/textvqa_r50_metrics.json --max-tokens 32 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --seed 0
note: v1 TextVQA @ 50% prune, REAL CLS-attn. OCR robustness gate for v1: proxy r50 was 0.530 (-2.5%, passed the <=5% gate). Expect v1 to hold or improve. This is the key OCR-degradation-resistance data point.

name: v1_probe_textvqa_r75
cmd: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate 0.75 --selector true_cls --benchmark textvqa --subset eval/subsets/textvqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_probe_v1/textvqa_r75_metrics.json --max-tokens 32 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --seed 0
note: v1 TextVQA @ 75% prune, REAL CLS-attn. Proxy r75 was 0.440 (-11.5%). The OCR stress test -- if true CLS-attn (which prioritizes text-region patches) recovers accuracy here, it directly demonstrates selector quality matters for OCR-heavy workloads.
